marketFocus.focus: Warsh Hawkish Fed Chair Nomination, Historic Metals Crash, Government Shutdown
headlines: Trump nominates Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair—hawkish on balance sheet, no rate cut commitment
Gold crashes -9% to sub-$5000, Silver -16%—historic precious metals sell-off continues
US partial government shutdown ongoing, equity futures at session lows
Iran-US tensions: Trump sends armada, Iran prepared for war/diplomacy
marketFocus.macroContext: Risk-off dominates as Warsh's hawkish Fed Chair nomination spooks markets already reeling from Friday's historic metals collapse. US government shutdown adds uncertainty. Hot US PPI (0.5% M/M vs 0.2% exp) reinforces no-cut narrative. Geopolitical premium rises with Iran-US standoff escalating.

tradingSummary: Defensive positioning prevails. USD bid on Warsh hawkish expectations despite partial shutdown. Commodities crushed—Gold/Silver in freefall, demand fears mount. EUR/GBP under pressure from weak PMI prints ahead. JPY gains safe-haven flows. Equities choppy, materials sector hammered. Focus shifts to ECB/BoE policy meetings later this week.

redFolderNews.event: UK S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Final

USD.summary: Warsh nomination injects hawkish Fed premium—balance sheet hawk, no rate cut signals. Hot PPI (0.5% M/M) kills dovish hopes. Partial government shutdown caps upside. DXY marginally soft but holding above key support. Iran tensions add safe-haven bid. Waller/Miran dissent highlights Fed split—Waller sees job market risk, Miran cites low inflation. Musalem says no more cuts, policy neutral. 1-3d: Choppy, Warsh rhetoric key.
headlines: Trump nominates Warsh as Fed Chair
USD.reaction: USD bid on Warsh hawkish expectations, but shutdown limits gains. Yields steepen.

EUR.summary: Weak PMI prints loom—Manufacturing PMIs (FRA/GER/EZ) due 09:50-10:00, likely soft. German CPI uptick (2.1% vs 2.0% exp) complicates ECB cut path but won't stop Feb easing. EZ GDP Q4 beat (1.3% vs 1.2%) marginal. EUR/USD sub-1.04, no support. ECB meeting Thu—25bps cut priced, Lagarde dovish tilt expected. Risk-off + weak data = EUR weakness. 1-3d: Downside bias, PMI misses amplify.
EUR.reaction: EUR pressured ahead of PMI prints, ECB cut expectations weigh.

GBP.summary: BoE meeting Thu—no cut expected, but dovish signals likely. UK PMI Final (10:30) key—soft print pressures GBP. Breeden (neutral-dovish) keynote 12:45 may hint at future easing. Cable choppy around 1.25, no clear direction. UK-Japan defence cooperation positive but no FX impact. Risk-off caps upside. Bailey/MPC rhetoric matters more than data. 1-3d: Range-bound, BoE preview dominates.
headlines: BoE meeting Thu, no cut expected
GBP.reaction: GBP steady but cautious ahead of BoE, PMI data watched.

JPY.summary: Risk-off = JPY strength. BoJ Summary of Opinions: One member says conditions accommodative post-hike. No intervention Dec 29-Jan 28 (MOF). Metals crash + equity weakness + Iran tensions drive safe-haven flows. USD/JPY dips as Warsh hawkish premium fades vs risk-off. Carry unwind accelerates. BoJ hike expectations (Mar/Apr) support JPY. 1-3d: Bullish, risk-off tailwind strong.
JPY.reaction: JPY rallies on safe-haven demand, risk-off dominates.

AUD.summary: Metals crash obliterates AUD—Gold -9%, Silver -16%, Copper/Iron Ore weak. China PMI 50.3 (in-line) offers no support. RBA hike odds (70%+) irrelevant vs commodity rout. Risk-off + demand fears = AUD crushed. AUD/USD sub-0.62, no floor. Materials sector hammered in equities. China stimulus hopes fading. 1-3d: Bearish, metals sell-off dominates.
AUD.reaction: AUD crushed by historic metals sell-off, risk-off amplifies.

CAD.summary: Trump threatens Canada with 25% tariff on aircrafts—USMCA tensions resurface. Crude flat/down (WTI $65.21, Brent $70.69), no oil support. BoC on hold, Macklem focused on tariff risks. Risk-off + tariff premium = CAD weakness. USD/CAD pushes higher. Metals crash (Gold/Silver) hits Canadian miners. 1-3d: Bearish, tariff rhetoric + risk-off weigh.
headlines: Trump threatens 25% tariff on Canadian aircrafts
CAD.reaction: CAD pressured by tariff threats, crude weakness adds to woes.

CHF.summary: Risk-off = CHF strength. Metals crash + equity weakness + geopolitical tensions (Iran-US) drive safe-haven flows. EUR/CHF dips as EUR weakness compounds. SNB dovish tilt (cut expectations) offset by risk-off demand. CHF outperforms vs commodity currencies (AUD/CAD). 1-3d: Bullish, safe-haven bid strong.
CHF.reaction: CHF rallies on safe-haven demand, risk-off dominates.

NZD.summary: Risk-off + commodity weakness = NZD crushed. Dairy prices soft, no support. China PMI 50.3 offers no relief. RBNZ cut expectations (Feb/Mar) weigh. NZD/USD sub-0.56, no floor. Carry unwind accelerates. Materials sector weakness (metals crash) hits NZ miners. 1-3d: Bearish, risk-off + commodity rout dominate.
NZD.reaction: NZD hammered by risk-off, commodity weakness amplifies.
